## [00:00 - 01:10]

What's the best way to talk to somebody new who you have nothing in common with? You do have something in common with them. They are human and you are human. You have something in common. Thanks for watching the video. I'm kidding, that would be a terrible video, but you see the way the thing is, I like to ask people questions. And while I'm asking people questions, what I'm hunting for is I'm hunting for the things that we may have in common. I want to share with you my top five favorite questions that I ask people that I've just met. The first one being, what's your favorite way to spend a full day off? The second thing, what's your bucket list location? Where would you really want to go and why? Number three, what are your favorite TV series that you'd like to binge? Now, if they say Game of Thrones, Scrubs, or they say Breaking Bad, we're going to become best friends. And if they say Neighbors or Home and Away, we can't be friends. The fourth one, if money were no object, what would you do? And the final one, what are the top three lessons you would tell your younger self? These are so much better than the common one we tend to reach for. And that's, so what do you do? Try those five I use and I promise you'll find something in common with the human being you're sitting across from.
